title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 164: AVOID These 3 Phone Habits That Are Killing Your Brain! The Mental Health Doctor: Dr Aditi Nerurkar
guest: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 164

Keep doingthat off and on until 4, 5 a.m. Your cortisol spike usually happens around 6 a.m., you know,give or take for people. That cortisol spike is a naturalthing that happens. What is a popcorn brain? Popcorn brain is an affliction thatnearly every single person has right now. Think about what you do when you're waiting in line atthe grocery store. Popcorn brain, on the other hand,is ubiquitous. It's everywhere. It is what defines modern life. Like that part of our brain that is focused on survivalStarting point is 00:04:32and self-preservation. We are no longer in a tribe. We're not cave people anymore. So what do we do? We scroll.That is how we are scanning for danger, especially when we are feeling stressed. In recent times,Starting point is 00:05:16there has been a lot of bad news. In fact, it feels like the onslaught of bad news, one thingafter another, whether it's a climate disaster or a conflict in a certain part of the world or something or the other is always happening now. The information stream, it israpid and unprecedented. And so we are constantly scrolling and scanning for danger. And it's thatprimal urge to scroll.
